title: crataegus oxyacantha proles mauriannensis
intro: Crataegus oxyacantha proles mauriannensis, also known as the hawthorn, is a subspecies of the Crataegus oxyacantha species, a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Europe and Western Asia. The medicinal properties of Crataegus oxyacantha proles mauriannensis include its high content of flavonoids, oligomeric proanthocyanidins, and other bioactive compounds that contribute to its cardiovascular benefits. The health benefits of hawthorn include reducing blood pressure, improving heart health, and potentially alleviating symptoms of heart failure, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Hawthorn is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its cardiovascular and antioxidant effects.
